MINI MINUTES: The following matters were called for hearing together:
1) PES-19-303368, The Estate of Thomas Iveli; and
2) PTR-24-307770 The Gaetano (Thomas) Iveli Revocable Trust.

Appearances:
Vincent K. Yu for executor and respondent Jon Vigna (via Zoom).
John D. Minton for creditors and petitioners Raymond P. Smith and Jonathan Knott (via Zoom).
Camille Colarusso, Objector in Pro Per (via Zoom).

The matters came on for hearing on 1) Petition for Relief Under Probate Code Section 850; 2) Amended Interim Account and Report of Executor; 3Petition for Order Compelling Accounting; and 4) Petition for Order Directing the Executor.

The Court stated it had reviewed the parties' stipulation. The Court further stated it was not prepared to adopt the stipulation as an order due to its informality, but was open to a short continuance and to addressing limited interim relief.

Mr. Yu requested a 60-day continuance. Mr. Minton requested a shorter continuance, noting that the estate and trust held approximately $3.5 million in liquid assets and an additional $1-2 million in real property, against a $17 million judgment. He stated that no distribution had been made to judgment creditors in over a year and requested $100,000 as an interim distribution.

Mr. Yu also requested advances of $50,000 to his office and $50,000 to Jon Vigna.

Ms. Colarusso objected, arguing that the judgment was void or unenforceable in probate, that the creditor's claim was time-barred, and that the judgment was procured by extrinsic fraud. The Court reminded Ms. Colarusso that the judgment would be treated as valid unless and until set aside.

Following discussion, the Court authorized disbursement of $100,000 to judgment creditors Raymond Smith and Jonathan Knott, over Ms. Colarusso's objection. The Court also authorized advances of $50,000 to Mr. Yu's office, on account of expected statutory probate fees, and $50,000 to Jon Vigna as an advance on his expected trustee's fees.

The Court continued Ms. Colarusso's Petition for Determination of Reversionary Interest, Determination of Heirship, and Transfer of Trust Property from October 21, 2025, to November 10, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. All other underlying petitions in the estate and trust matters were also continued to the same date.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

MINI MINUTES: The following cases were called and heard together:
1) PES-19-303368, Estate of Thomas Iveli; and
2) PTR-24-307770, Gaetano (Thomas) Iveli Revocable Trust

Appearances:
John D. Minton and Asher S. Anderson for Raymond P. Smith and Jonathan Knott, Petitioners (via Zoom).
Vincent Yu for Jon Vigna, Executor (via Zoom).
Camille Colarusso, Interested Party (via Zoom).

In the trust matter, the Court stated that it accepted venue as proper in San Francisco based on the material submitted. Ms. Colarusso objected, citing improper notice and due process concerns related to earlier proceedings in San Mateo County. The Court invited her to submit written objections.

Mr. Anderson argued the section 850 petition was necessary due to confusion over property ownership between the decedent and Robert Sigmund. They asserted a conflict of interest in Mr. Vigna's role as fiduciary for both estates and questioned his neutrality. Mr. Yu disagreed, stating his office was still reviewing documents and that it was premature to adjudicate the 850 petition.

Regarding the petition to direct the executor, Mr. Minton emphasized petitioners' creditor status following a jury verdict and expressed concern over delays and diminishing estate assets. They requested an interim distribution plan. Mr. Yu responded that unresolved legal and financial matters made distributions premature but agreed to confer on next steps.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to September 8, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, with direction for the parties to meet and confer on potential interim distribution and to address outstanding accounting and notice issues.

Off Calendar Jan-18-2023 Continued To Feb-15-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom: Vincent Yu, Esq. for Jon Vigna (Executor/Trustee). Camille Colarusso (Niece) in propria persona. Joseph Iveli (Decedent's Brother) in propria persona. Court-Appointed Counsel Rowena Navia for Robert Sigmund (Beneficiary of the Trusts/Conservatee in PCN-19-303294). Virginia Palmer, Esq. for Raymond Smith (Conservator of the Person of Robert J. Sigmund in PCN-19-303294). Avid Alavi, Esq. for Bennett Hong (Successor Conservator of the Estate of Robert J. Sigmund in PCN-20-303294).

The Court inquired about bond. Mr. Yu noted that the Court had requested a $20 million dollar bond, stated that an ex parte is ready to be filed to reduce the bond to $13 million dollars, that he has been speaking with the bonding company and that the matter was escalated to a supervisor, and that bond is currently set at $2.5 million dollars. The Court inquired about the inventory and appraisal (I&A). Mr. Yu stated that it should be a final I&A, that the filing requires a wet signature from his client, that he has sent the filing via Fed Ex to his client but it keeps coming back, that the confusion comes from his client's residence being located above a business, that his client confirmed that he is at that residence, and that they haven't had this much of a problem before when using Fed Ex. The Court addressed Mr. Yu stating that perhaps he should consider DHL or the postal service, that everyone is here on a multi-million dollar issue, and inquired if the Court should issue an order to show cause to remove his client. Mr. Yu stated that he understood the Court's position and that removal is not necessary. Ms. Colarusso stated that a P.O. Box would assist with the Fed Ex issue noting that there was no need for the back and forth. Ms. Palmer presented brief argument in support of the need for a professional to manage the estate noting that this is over Mr. Vigna's head. Ms. Colarusso stated that she did not want another attorney coming in noting that the funds are depleting. Ms. Alavi stated that the I&A could be emailed to Mr. Yu's client with instructions to mail the I&A back to Mr. Yu. The Court inquired about there being attorney services available in New York. Mr. Yu responded stating that an attorney was hired to help his client corral things on his end and they are in communication with that attorney, that his office has not taken a dime or penny, and that they use their charm to get people to work with their office. In addition, Mr. Yu stated that that there is so much information, addressed the liquidity issue, stated that there is no accountant until they can get cash into the estate/trust, that they should be filing by the end of next week, and provided a brief update regarding the Baker Street property, etc. The Court entertained comments about a $13 million dollar bond. Ms. Palmer stated that she did not have enough information. Ms. Colarruso stated that $13 million was better than $2.5 million. Ms. Navia agreed with Ms. Palmer. Mr. Yu addressed the liquidity issue again stating that once that's in place things will roll, that they have gotten a lot done, commented on his firm's ability to corral equity using their charm, and noted that they were able to get things done by standing with their client. The Court stated that it did not want to hear a ridiculous excuse regarding a signature, that the matter was on 11/9/22, that counsel represented then that the I&A was coming, that a $20 million dollar bond was ordered, and that it will go back and take a look at whether it should issue an OSC.

The status on interim accounting and status on filing the final inventory and appraisal are continued to February 15, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. The Court addressed Mr. Yu stating that it was awaiting more information on the bond and that he should be more forthcoming with the math and why the bond amount is correct.

Ms. Colarusso stated that the last Attorney who was going to take the case has retired and recommended another Attorney who is asking for a $100,000.00 retainer, that it is not feasible for her, that she has prepared her objections with new documents and that she filed everything she was supposed to file. Ms. Colarusso also stated that the Court received the documents yesterday, that they have not posted yet and that everyone will be receiving them. The Court informed Ms. Colarusso that her petition was rejected. Ms. Zulaica stated that despite the objection she believes the Court should grant her petition and admit the will, that the objection is based on a promise, that they have a piece of paper with scribbles on it and that it is a creditor's claim. Ms. Zulaica also stated that there is continued confusion about what a proper objection is, that the grounds to objecting are based on a misunderstanding of the law, that Ms. Colarusso has been given ample time to hire Counsel and that the objections have been procedurally defective. In addition, Ms. Zulaica stated that they have done their due diligence to try and locate other wills, that there are none, that the 1983 and the 1989 documents do the same thing, that the parties have 120-days to revoke the will after it is admitted and that the parties still have recourse. Furthermore, Ms. Zulaica stated that if a will is discovered, they will have to bring it to the Court, that this is a large estate, that it is a complicated estate, that the tax return is due next month, that there are tenant issues, that there are corporation issues and that they need to have someone in place. Ms. Robello and Ms. Palmer both concur with Ms. Zulaica's comments/assessments. The Court stated that it recognizes Ms. Colarusso's issues, but it can't delay this any longer. The special letters are extended September 21, 2020. The Amended Petition for Probate is continued to September 21, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If Ms. Colarusso has no Attorney and no action is taken, the Court intends to approve Ms. Zulaica's petition.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Reporter: Teresa Kenworthy, CSR #6673, 530-217-3811, tkenworthy@gmail.com

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jun-24-2020 Continued To Aug-19-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sheila Robello appeared by telephone for Jon Vigna (Proposed Co-Executor).
Yulissa Zulaica appeared by telephone for Michael Rooney (Petitioner/Proposed Co-Executor).
Virginia Palmer appeared by telephone for and with Raymond Smith (Conservator) by telephone.
Camille Colarusso appeared by telephone in Propria Persona.

Ms. Colarusso stated that she is intending to pursue a will contest, that she filed an answer to the ex parte petition, but it is not posted yet and that she has been contacting Attorneys. Ms. Colarusso also stated that due to Covid Attorneys are not at full strength and are not taking new cases, but she hopes to have Counsel by the end of this week. The Court informed Ms. Colarusso that what was filed does not comply with the Probate Code, that no summons was issued or served on the appropriate people and noted that the petition was filed in December. Counsel expressed that Ms. Colarusso has had sufficient time to obtain Counsel and asked to have a short continuance if the Court is inclined to do so. Ms. Zulaica asked the Court to set a deadline and stated that no legal grounds have been presented that would not allow this petition to move forward. Ms. Robello stated that the estate has been significantly delayed and raised the issue of the 706 deadlines. Ms. Colarusso stated that Raymond, John and Michael are all Friends, that the estate needs to be protected, raised the issue of the creditor's claims and stated that she feels a Neutral needs to be appointed. Ms. Colarusso also stated that her Uncles medical issues were hidden/kept from her, that certain things have not been done properly and that she has been prejudiced against. Ms. Zulaica stated that John Knott was a Co-Conservator of the Decedent before he passed away, that nobody is in a legal position to address any of the creditor's claims at this point, and that any challenge to this will will not be successful as the prior wills do the same and Ms. Colarusso is not named. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary is continued to August 19, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for issuance of a summons and for proper service. The Court granted the Ex Parte Petition to Extend Special Letters (subject to review of the order). The special letters are extended to August 19, 2020.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Anasse. Not reported.
